person.name: Countess Adelaide Adelisa de Soissons de-Bar-Sur-Seine

nameForms.fullText: Countess Adelaide Adelisa de Soissons de-Bar-Sur-Seine

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
geni.com Adelaide de Soissons Also Known As: "Alice", "Adelisa", "Adelais" Birthdate: between circa 954 and 988 Birthplace: Soissons, Aisne, Nord-Pas-de-Calais-Picardie, France Death: March 31, 1047 (58-97) Bar Sur Aube, Aube, Alsace-Champagne-Ardenne-Lorraine, France Immediate Family: Daughter of Giselbert, comte de Soissons? and Wife of Giselbert Wife of Nocher I de la Ferté-sir-Aube, count of Bar-sur Aube and Guy I de Vermandois, comte de Soissons Mother of Renaud de Vermandois, comte de Soissons
LESS



Adelisa, daughter of Guy I, Count of Soissons, and his wife Adelisa. Countess of Soissons, inherited from her father as his only child. Adelisa is frequently confused with her mother.

Adelisa married Nocher II, Count of Bar-sur-Aube. (Some sources claim that Nocher was possibly the second husband of the widow of Guy I.) Adelisa and Nocher had at least one child:

«b»Renaud I, Count of Soissons«/b»
Nocher II became Count of Soissons, jure uxoris, upon his marriage to Adelisa, but apparently died in 1019, at which point Adelisa ruled until Renaud was of age.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Countess Adelaide Adelisa de Soissons de-Bar-Sur-Seine於0966年5月9日出生在法國皮卡迪艾斯索頌斯時，她的父親Comte de Soissons Guy I de Vermandois18歲，母親Agnes d’Anjou28歲。她於大約0990年與Nocher de la Ferté-sir-Aube I, count of Bar-sur Aube結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1047年3月31日在法國德 艾斯特奧布巴-素爾-奧布享年80歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Soissons
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: from a variant of Old French soçon, sosson ‘companion, partner, workmate’ (from Latin socius), hence a nickname for a laborer, perhaps one who usually worked alongside a horse or ox.

nameMeanings.name: Adelaide
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic origin (via French Adélaïde ), from adal ‘noble’ + heid ‘kind, sort’. It was borne in the 10th century by the wife of the Holy Roman Emperor Otto the Great. She became regent after his death and was revered as a saint. The given name increased in popularity in England during the 19th century, when it was borne by the wife of King William IV; she was the daughter of the ruler of the German duchy of Saxe-Meiningen. The Australian city of Adelaide was named in her honour.

nameMeanings.name: Adelisa
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Adeline  , which has enjoyed some favour in the English-speaking world. It was famously borne by the Spanish-born opera singer  Adelina Pattie  (  1843–1919 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Countess Adelaide Adelisa de Soissons de-Bar-Sur-Seine was born on 9 May 0966, in Soissons, Aisne, Picardie, France, her father, Comte de Soissons Guy I de Vermandois, was 18 and her mother, Agnes d’Anjou, was 28. She married Nocher de la Ferté-sir-Aube I, count of Bar-sur Aube about 0990. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 31 March 1047, in Bar-sur-Aube, Aube, Grand Est, France, at the age of 80.

Elle a épousé Nocher de la Ferté-sir-Aube I, count of Bar-sur Aube environ 0990. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 31 mars 1047, à Bar-sur-Aube, Aube, Grand Est, France, à l’âge de 80 ans.
